questionHtml:
Just before the loop is about to completely exit the region of magnetic field, the loop moves with constant terminal speed v.

The following data is available:

Mass of loop m = 4.0 g
Resistance of loop R = 25 mΩ
Width of loop L = 15 cm
Magnetic flux density B = 0.80 T

Determine, in m s−1 the terminal speed v.
markschemeHtml:
mg = BIL  OR  I = 0.33 «A» ✓

BvL = IR  OR  ℰ = 8.25 × 10−3 «V»  OR  ℰ = 0.12v ✓

Combining results to get v = mgRB2L2 ✓

v = «0.040×9.81×0.0250.802×0.152 =» 0.068 «m s−1»

Allow ECF between steps if clear work is shown.

meta.title: Just before the loop is about to completely exit the region of magnetic field, the loop moves with constant terminal speed v. The following data is available: Mass of loop m = 4.0
breadcrumbLabels: B. The particulate nature of matter
